questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Baffle.”
questions.explanation: A) Baffle (verb) – To totally bewilder or perplex; to restrain or regulate the flow of a fluid or sound. (चकित करना/असमंजस में डालना) Synonym: Confound (verb) – To cause surprise or confusion in someone, especially by acting against their expectations. (चकित करना) • Capitulate (verb): To cease to resist an opponent or an unwelcome demand; to surrender. (आत्मसमर्पण करना) • Castigate (verb): To reprimand someone severely; to punish or criticize someone harshly. (कड़ी आलोचना करना) • Corroborate (verb): To confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding. (पुष्टि करना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate MEANING of the noun “Patior.”
options: To endure, undergo, or suffer a condition
To endorse, support, or approve a proposal
To enchant, fascinate, or charm a bystander
To encroach, trespass, or intrude a property
questions.explanation: A) Patior (noun/root) – A Latin root meaning to suffer, endure, or permit; the foundation for words like "patience" and "passion." (सहन करना)

questions.question: Which of the following idioms given in the alternatives has the meaning? To change or reverse something dramatically.
questions.explanation: A) Turn the tide (idiom) – To change or reverse something dramatically स्थिति को पूरी तरह बदल देना • Buck the tide (idiom) – To go against the trend or resist opposition धारा के विपरीत जाना • Swim with the tide (idiom) – To follow the general trend or go along with others धारा के साथ चलना • Tide one over (idiom) – To help someone through a difficult time कठिन समय में सहारा देना

questions.question: Which of the following idioms given in the alternatives has the meaning? A person who is strong, resilient, and not easily defeated or discouraged in difficult situations.
questions.explanation: A) Tough cookie (idiom) – A person who is strong, resilient, and not easily discouraged मजबूत और हिम्मती व्यक्ति • Tough cheese (idiom) – Used to express lack of sympathy; “that’s your bad luck” कोई सहानुभूति नहीं • Tough sledding (idiom) – A difficult situation कठिन परिस्थिति • Tough row to hoe (idiom) – A hard task or challenging situation कठिन काम

questions.question: Choose the word that is most opposite in meaning to “destruction”.
questions.explanation: A) Destruction (noun) – Ruin, devastation, demolition, annihilation, damage विनाश Antonym: Preservation (noun) – Protection, conservation, safeguarding, maintenance, saving संरक्षण • Devastation (noun) – Great destruction, ruin, havoc, wreckage तबाही • Ruin (noun) – Destruction, collapse, downfall, devastation विनाश • Damage (noun) – Harm, injury, destruction, loss नुकसान

questions.question: Choose the word that is the antonym of Immigration.
questions.explanation: B) Immigration (noun) – Entry into a country, influx, arrival, settlement आगमन / देश में आकर बसना Antonym: Emigration (noun) – Leaving one’s country, departure, exit, migration out देश छोड़कर जाना / प्रवासन • Influx (noun) – Arrival, inflow, entry, coming in आगमन • Entry (noun) – Admission, access, arrival, ingress प्रवेश • Arrival (noun) – Coming, entry, appearance, advent आगमन

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. A person who is well known for their bad deeds.
questions.explanation: D) Notorious (adjective) – Famous for bad deeds कुख्यात • Indolent (adjective) – Lazy, avoiding work आलसी • Versatile (adjective) – Having many skills बहुमुखी • Eminent (adjective) – Famous and respected प्रतिष्ठित

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. A mental condition where a person feels an uncontrollable urge to steal.
questions.explanation: B) Kleptomania: An inability to resist the urge to steal items, usually for reasons other than personal use or financial gain. Pyromania: An obsessive desire to set fire to things. Bibliomania: An extreme passion for collecting or possessing books. Anglomania: An excessive admiration for English customs, culture, or people.

questions.question: There are four underlined words in the sentence given below. One of them is misspelt. Choose the option with the misspelt word. Mumbai’s new elevated nature walkway preserves indigenious fauna and flora while ensuring minimal intervention during its eco-friendly construction.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling of ‘indigenious’ is ‘indigenous’, which means “native to a particular place” स्थानीय, मूल निवासी।

questions.question: Choose the misspelt word from the given options.
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling of ‘Opulance’ is ‘Opulence’, which means “great wealth or luxury” वैभव, ऐश्वर्य।

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into Passive Voice? Did the Romans defeat the Greeks?
options: Was the Greeks defeated by the Romans?
Did the Greeks defeated by the Romans?
Are the Greeks defeated by the Romans?
Were the Greeks defeated by the Romans?
questions.explanation: D) Were the Greeks defeated by the Romans? Active से Passive Voice में बदलने के लिए, Active वाक्य का Object Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Verb को Active से Passive में बदलते समय सही Tense का प्रयोग किया जाता है। यहाँ "the Romans" Subject है, "did defeat" (Past Simple, interrogative form) Verb है और "the Greeks" Object है। Passive में बदलने पर Object "the Greeks" Subject बन जाता है, Verb को "were defeated" (Past Simple Passive) में बदला जाता है और "by the Romans" जोड़ा जाता है। साथ ही, प्रश्नवाचक संरचना बनाए रखने के लिए "Were" से वाक्य शुरू होता है। इसलिए सही वाक्य है — "Were the Greeks defeated by the Romans?" To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence. The verb is changed into the passive form with the correct tense. In this case, "the Romans" (Subject), "did defeat" (Verb in Past Simple interrogative form), and "the Greeks" (Object) are transformed into "the Greeks" (Subject), "were defeated" (Passive Verb in Past Simple), and "by the Romans". The interrogative structure is maintained by starting with "Were". Hence, the correct answer is — "Were the Greeks defeated by the Romans?" (Option 4)

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into active voice? Were the examination papers being checked by the teachers until late at night?
options: Are the teachers checking the examination papers until late at night?
Were the teachers checking the examination papers until late at night?
Did the teachers check the examination papers until late at night?
Have the teachers checked the examination papers until late at night?
questions.explanation: B) Were the teachers checking the examination papers until late at night? Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject Active वाक्य का Object बन जाता है और "by + doer" Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय सही Tense का ध्यान रखा जाता है। यहाँ "the examination papers" Subject है, "were being checked" (Past Continuous Passive) Verb है और "by the teachers" doer है। Active में बदलने पर "the teachers" Subject बन जाता है, Verb "were checking" (Past Continuous) हो जाता है और "the examination papers" Object बन जाता है। साथ ही, प्रश्नवाचक संरचना बनाए रखने के लिए "Were" से वाक्य शुरू होता है। इसलिए सही वाक्य है — "Were the teachers checking the examination papers until late at night?" To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the subject of the passive sentence becomes the object, and the doer (introduced by "by") becomes the subject. The verb is converted into the correct active tense. In this case, "the examination papers" (Subject), "were being checked" (Past Continuous Passive), and "by the teachers" are transformed into "the teachers" (Subject), "were checking" (Active Verb in Past Continuous), and "the examination papers" (Object). The interrogative form is maintained by starting with "Were". Hence, the correct answer is — "Were the teachers checking the examination papers until late at night?" (Option 2)

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into active voice? Couldn’t the cancer be stopped from spreading further by the doctors?
options: Couldn’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further?
Can’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further?
Won’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further?
Shouldn’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further?
questions.explanation: A) Couldn’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further? Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject Active वाक्य का Object बन जाता है और "by + doer" Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय सही Modal और Tense का प्रयोग किया जाता है। यहाँ "the cancer" Subject है, "couldn’t be stopped" (Modal Passive) Verb है और "by the doctors" doer है। Active में बदलने पर "the doctors" Subject बन जाता है, Verb "couldn’t stop" (Modal Active) हो जाता है और "the cancer" Object बन जाता है। साथ ही, प्रश्नवाचक संरचना बनाए रखने के लिए "Couldn’t" से वाक्य शुरू होता है। इसलिए सही वाक्य है — "Couldn’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further?" To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the subject of the passive sentence becomes the object, and the doer (introduced by "by") becomes the subject. The verb is converted into the correct active form, keeping the modal unchanged. In this case, "the cancer" (Subject), "couldn’t be stopped" (Modal Passive), and "by the doctors" are transformed into "the doctors" (Subject), "couldn’t stop" (Active Verb with Modal), and "the cancer" (Object). The interrogative form is maintained by starting with "Couldn’t". Hence, the correct answer is — "Couldn’t the doctors stop the cancer from spreading further?" (Option 1)

questions.question: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence. The writer often used clever _______ to hint at deeper meanings.
questions.explanation: A) 'Allusions' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि sentence में writer के द्वारा “hint at deeper meanings” यानी गहरे अर्थों की ओर संकेत करने की बात हो रही है। “Allusion” का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ का अप्रत्यक्ष या संकेतात्मक उल्लेख करना, जो यहाँ perfectly fit बैठता है। इसलिए, "allusions" सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प होगा। • ‘Allusions’ should be used because the sentence talks about hinting at deeper meanings. An “allusion” means an indirect reference or hint toward something, which fits perfectly in this context. Thus, "allusions" would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Select the correct alternative to fill in the blank with the correct preposition in the following sentence. You will find the mosque ________ the right walking straight along this road.
questions.explanation: A) on’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि जब हम किसी सड़क पर चलते हुए किसी स्थान (जैसे building, mosque, shop आदि) की दिशा बताते हैं, तो “on the right / on the left” का प्रयोग किया जाता है। यहाँ sentence में बताया जा रहा है कि सीधे चलते हुए दाईं तरफ मस्जिद मिलेगी, इसलिए “on the right” सही प्रयोग है। • ‘on’ should be used because while giving directions along a road, we use the expression “on the right / on the left” to indicate position. Here, the sentence tells that the mosque will be located on the right side while walking straight, so “on” is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: The word "match" is a homonym. In which sentence does it mean something used to light a fire?
options: The match between her dress and shoes was perfect.
The cricket match was postponed due to rain.
He struck a match to light the candle.
They are a good match for each other.
questions.explanation: C) He struck a match to light the candle. Sentence 3 में “match” का अर्थ है तीली (a stick used to light fire) — सही उत्तर। In Sentence 3, “match” means a stick used to light a fire — correct answer.

questions.question: A part of the following sentence is underlined. Choose the option that can replace the underlined part to improve the sentence. If you feel Options A, B, or C do not improve the sentence, then choose Option D: “No improvement required.” We must take the bull by the head and resolve all pending issues if we want progress.
options: take the bull by the tail
pull the bull by its ears
take the bull by the horns
questions.explanation: C) take the bull by the head’ के बदले ‘take the bull by the horns’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यह एक सही और प्रचलित idiom है, जिसका अर्थ होता है किसी समस्या का साहसपूर्वक और सीधे सामना करना। वाक्य में “resolve all pending issues” दिया गया है, जो सीधे समस्या का सामना करने का भाव देता है, इसलिए यही idiom उपयुक्त है। • ‘take the bull by the horns’ will be used instead of ‘take the bull by the head’ because it is the correct idiomatic expression meaning to deal with a problem boldly and directly; Like— We must take the bull by the horns to solve this crisis.

questions.question: The following sentence is divided into four parts and are given in the alternatives A, B, C, and D. Select that alternative which contains that part of the sentence which has an error. Selecting the part with the error is the answer. He figured that to find a decent drum he had to go to a large flea market that is in a major city and usually gives place on weekends.
options: He figured that to find a decent
drum he had to go to a large flea
market that is in a major city and
questions.explanation: D) gives place' के बदले 'takes place' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ अर्थ है कि flea market “होता है / आयोजित होता है”, न कि “जगह देता है”। • 'takes place' will be used instead of 'gives place' because the correct expression for an event happening is “takes place”, not “gives place”. Correct Sentence: He figured that to find a decent drum he had to go to a large flea market that is in a major city and usually takes place on weekends.

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences in correct order to make a meaningful sentence: P: while the committee deliberated on the proposed reforms Q: numerous stakeholders voiced their concerns R: regarding the potential impacts S: during the public consultation process
questions.explanation: B) QPRS. • The logical flow is: Q introduces the main clause (stakeholders voicing concerns); P adds a concurrent subordinate clause (while the committee deliberated); R specifies what the concerns were about (regarding the potential impacts); S provides the overall context of when this took place (during the public consultation process). A (RSPQ) is incorrect – begins with ‘regarding the potential impacts’ which is a dependent phrase and cannot open the sentence meaningfully. C (QSRP) is incorrect – places ‘during the public consultation process’ before ‘regarding the potential impacts’, disrupting the logical connection between the concerns and their context. D (PRQS) is incorrect – begins with the subordinate clause P and ends with Q (main clause), reversing the natural subject-first structure.

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences in correct order to make a meaningful passage: 1. The team submitted the compiled data to the analytics division. 2. Raw survey responses were extracted from multiple sources. 3. Each dataset underwent preprocessing and standardization. 4. Redundant entries were eliminated using automated filters.
questions.explanation: A) 2-3-4-1. • The logical flow follows a data processing pipeline: first raw data is extracted (2), then each dataset is cleaned through preprocessing and standardization (3), then redundant entries are removed using filters (4), and finally the compiled and cleaned data is submitted to the analytics division (1). B (2-4-3-1) is incorrect – places elimination of redundant entries (4) before preprocessing (3), which is illogical since you must first preprocess before filtering redundancies. C (3-2-4-1) is incorrect – begins with preprocessing (3) before raw data extraction (2) has taken place, which reverses the logical order. D (1-3-2-4) is incorrect – begins with submission of compiled data (1) which is the final step and cannot logically come first.

questions.question: Comprehension: Iron and calcium are two indispensable minerals required for the healthy growth of the human body, especially in children and women. Iron plays a crucial role in the production of hemoglobin, the pigment that transports oxygen from the lungs to various tissues. A deficiency of iron often leads to anemia, which is characterized by fatigue, dizziness, and a weakened immune system. In children, this deficiency may also result in delayed cognitive development and poor academic performance. Calcium, on the other hand, is primarily associated with the formation and maintenance of strong bones and teeth. Inadequate calcium intake during childhood hampers skeletal growth, while in women it increases the risk of osteoporosis a condition where bones become porous and fragile. Pregnant and lactating mothers require additional calcium, as their bodies support not only their own needs but also those of the growing child. The causes of these deficiencies are often linked to poor dietary habits, lack of awareness, and socio-economic factors. Diets deficient in green leafy vegetables, dairy products, and iron-rich foods such as pulses and lean meat significantly contribute to the problem. Therefore, it becomes imperative to encourage balanced nutrition, timely medical check-ups, and awareness campaigns. Only then can society combat the silent yet widespread issue of iron and calcium deficiency, ensuring healthier generations in the future. Identify the correct verb form in the sentence. “Calcium, on the other hand, ___ primarily associated with the formation and maintenance of strong bones and teeth.”
questions.explanation: D) 'is' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि subject “Calcium” एक uncountable singular noun है, इसलिए इसके साथ singular verb ही प्रयोग होगा। वाक्य सामान्य तथ्य (general fact) को दर्शा रहा है, इसलिए present tense का प्रयोग उचित है। अतः “Calcium … is primarily associated…” सही होगा। • ‘is’ should be used because the subject “Calcium” is an uncountable singular noun, so it takes a singular verb. The sentence expresses a general fact, hence present tense is appropriate. Therefore, “Calcium … is primarily associated…” is correct.

questions.question: Comprehension: Iron and calcium are two indispensable minerals required for the healthy growth of the human body, especially in children and women. Iron plays a crucial role in the production of hemoglobin, the pigment that transports oxygen from the lungs to various tissues. A deficiency of iron often leads to anemia, which is characterized by fatigue, dizziness, and a weakened immune system. In children, this deficiency may also result in delayed cognitive development and poor academic performance. Calcium, on the other hand, is primarily associated with the formation and maintenance of strong bones and teeth. Inadequate calcium intake during childhood hampers skeletal growth, while in women it increases the risk of osteoporosis a condition where bones become porous and fragile. Pregnant and lactating mothers require additional calcium, as their bodies support not only their own needs but also those of the growing child. The causes of these deficiencies are often linked to poor dietary habits, lack of awareness, and socio-economic factors. Diets deficient in green leafy vegetables, dairy products, and iron-rich foods such as pulses and lean meat significantly contribute to the problem. Therefore, it becomes imperative to encourage balanced nutrition, timely medical check-ups, and awareness campaigns. Only then can society combat the silent yet widespread issue of iron and calcium deficiency, ensuring healthier generations in the future. Identify the type of sentence. “A deficiency of iron often leads to anemia.”
questions.explanation: C) Declarative' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यह वाक्य एक सामान्य तथ्य या जानकारी को स्पष्ट रूप से व्यक्त कर रहा है। इसमें न तो कोई प्रश्न पूछा गया है, न कोई आदेश दिया गया है, और न ही कोई भावनात्मक विस्मय व्यक्त किया गया है। इसलिए यह एक कथनात्मक (statement) वाक्य है। • ‘Declarative’ should be used because the sentence clearly states a fact or information. It neither asks a question, nor gives a command, nor expresses strong emotion. Hence, it is a declarative sentence.

questions.question: Comprehension: Iron and calcium are two indispensable minerals required for the healthy growth of the human body, especially in children and women. Iron plays a crucial role in the production of hemoglobin, the pigment that transports oxygen from the lungs to various tissues. A deficiency of iron often leads to anemia, which is characterized by fatigue, dizziness, and a weakened immune system. In children, this deficiency may also result in delayed cognitive development and poor academic performance. Calcium, on the other hand, is primarily associated with the formation and maintenance of strong bones and teeth. Inadequate calcium intake during childhood hampers skeletal growth, while in women it increases the risk of osteoporosis a condition where bones become porous and fragile. Pregnant and lactating mothers require additional calcium, as their bodies support not only their own needs but also those of the growing child. The causes of these deficiencies are often linked to poor dietary habits, lack of awareness, and socio-economic factors. Diets deficient in green leafy vegetables, dairy products, and iron-rich foods such as pulses and lean meat significantly contribute to the problem. Therefore, it becomes imperative to encourage balanced nutrition, timely medical check-ups, and awareness campaigns. Only then can society combat the silent yet widespread issue of iron and calcium deficiency, ensuring healthier generations in the future. According to the passage, why do “pregnant and lactating mothers require additional calcium”?
options: Because of their role in household work
Because their bodies support both their own needs and those of the growing child
questions.explanation: C) 'Because their bodies support both their own needs and those of the growing child' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि गर्भवती और स्तनपान कराने वाली माताओं के शरीर को अपने साथ-साथ बच्चे की भी आवश्यकताओं को पूरा करना होता है। इसलिए उन्हें अतिरिक्त calcium की आवश्यकता होती है। • ‘Because their bodies support both their own needs and those of the growing child’ should be used because the passage clearly states that pregnant and lactating mothers need to meet not only their own nutritional needs but also those of the developing child. Hence, they require additional calcium.

questions.question: Comprehension; Iron and calcium are two indispensable minerals required for the healthy growth of the human body, especially in children and women. Iron plays a crucial role in the production of hemoglobin, the pigment that transports oxygen from the lungs to various tissues. A deficiency of iron often leads to anemia, which is characterized by fatigue, dizziness, and a weakened immune system. In children, this deficiency may also result in delayed cognitive development and poor academic performance. Calcium, on the other hand, is primarily associated with the formation and maintenance of strong bones and teeth. Inadequate calcium intake during childhood hampers skeletal growth, while in women it increases the risk of osteoporosis a condition where bones become porous and fragile. Pregnant and lactating mothers require additional calcium, as their bodies support not only their own needs but also those of the growing child. The causes of these deficiencies are often linked to poor dietary habits, lack of awareness, and socio-economic factors. Diets deficient in green leafy vegetables, dairy products, and iron-rich foods such as pulses and lean meat significantly contribute to the problem. Therefore, it becomes imperative to encourage balanced nutrition, timely medical check-ups, and awareness campaigns. Only then can society combat the silent yet widespread issue of iron and calcium deficiency, ensuring healthier generations in the future. The word ‘indispensable’ in the sentence “Iron and calcium are two indispensable minerals required for the healthy growth of the human body…” means _______.
questions.explanation: B) 'essential' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि “indispensable” का अर्थ होता है ऐसा जो अत्यंत आवश्यक हो और जिसके बिना काम न चल सके। यहाँ वाक्य में iron और calcium को शरीर के स्वस्थ विकास के लिए जरूरी बताया गया है, इसलिए “essential” सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है। • ‘essential’ should be used because “indispensable” means something that is absolutely necessary and cannot be done without. In the sentence, iron and calcium are described as necessary for healthy growth, so “essential” is the correct choice.

questions.question: Comprehension: Iron and calcium are two indispensable minerals required for the healthy growth of the human body, especially in children and women. Iron plays a crucial role in the production of hemoglobin, the pigment that transports oxygen from the lungs to various tissues. A deficiency of iron often leads to anemia, which is characterized by fatigue, dizziness, and a weakened immune system. In children, this deficiency may also result in delayed cognitive development and poor academic performance. Calcium, on the other hand, is primarily associated with the formation and maintenance of strong bones and teeth. Inadequate calcium intake during childhood hampers skeletal growth, while in women it increases the risk of osteoporosis a condition where bones become porous and fragile. Pregnant and lactating mothers require additional calcium, as their bodies support not only their own needs but also those of the growing child. The causes of these deficiencies are often linked to poor dietary habits, lack of awareness, and socio-economic factors. Diets deficient in green leafy vegetables, dairy products, and iron-rich foods such as pulses and lean meat significantly contribute to the problem. Therefore, it becomes imperative to encourage balanced nutrition, timely medical check-ups, and awareness campaigns. Only then can society combat the silent yet widespread issue of iron and calcium deficiency, ensuring healthier generations in the future. In the sentence “…in women it increases the risk of osteoporosis—a condition where bones become porous and fragile,” the antonym of ‘fragile’ is _______.
questions.explanation: D) Fragile (adjective) – Weak, delicate, brittle, breakable, frail नाज़ुक / कमजोर Antonym: Strong (adjective) – Sturdy, tough, durable, firm, powerful मजबूत • Weak (adjective) – Fragile, feeble, frail कमजोर • Delicate (adjective) – Fragile, fine, sensitive नाज़ुक • Brittle (adjective) – Fragile, breakable, crisp भंगुर
